methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Solid Power is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector that has raised $232M to date. The valuation universe for direct peers is constrained: while 19 companies operate in Climate & Energy broadly, the narrow peer set—filtered by stage, adjacency, and valuation tier—yielded only 2 same-stage comparables, falling short of the 3-datapoint minimum needed for robust peer-median analysis. The comp universe includes 1 public comparable (LanzaTech, ticker LNZA) and 8 private peers, among them QuantumScape, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion.

Given the sparsity of narrow-stage peers, peer-median triangulation is not applicable. The small public comp set (n=1) is insufficient to establish a defensible public-market multiple baseline. Similarly, the private peer set, while larger, does not yield enough same-stage or adjacent-stage datapoints to anchor valuation via revenue or EBITDA multiples with confidence.

This analysis underscores a core uncertainty: Solid Power operates in a subsector (solid-state battery technology and related energy infrastructure) where direct comparables at its stage and scale are limited. Any valuation conclusion would require either: (1) expansion to broader Climate & Energy multiples with explicit stage and risk discounts applied, or (2) reliance on alternative methods (DCF, sum-of-the-parts) not available in the current dataset. Current public market data alone cannot support a triangulated range without methodological caveats.
